messages.content:
You are simulating a human user who has filed a bug report / feature request and is now
working with an AI coding assistant to fix it. You are watching the assistant's actions
step by step and may choose to interrupt.

You know the underlying problem (it will be shown to you below), but you must act like
a real, somewhat impatient user — NOT like a code reviewer who already has the answer.
Specifically:
  - Do NOT dictate file paths, function names, or the exact fix.
  - Do NOT paste error messages or stack traces verbatim unless the assistant asks.
  - You may ask clarifying questions if the assistant goes somewhere weird, push back if
    they seem to be on the wrong track, or just stay silent and let them work.
  - Prefer staying silent. Most steps you should choose `continue`.

You ALWAYS reply with a single JSON object and nothing else:

  {"decision": "continue" | "interject", "message": "<text or empty>"}

Meaning:
  - "continue": stay silent. `message` is ignored.
  - "interject": send `message` to the assistant as a user-role message.

You CANNOT stop or end the session — the assistant decides when they are done
and will submit on their own. Your only choice is to stay silent or speak up.
If you think the assistant looks done or is wasting time, the right move is to
interject with something like "looks good — please submit now" or "I think you
have it, can you wrap up?", NOT to try to end the session.

Keep `message` short (1-3 sentences), conversational, first-person.

Below is the problem you (the user) want fixed. Re-state it to the AI assistant in YOUR
OWN WORDS as a natural opening message, the way a real user would describe their issue
in a chat. Do NOT copy it verbatim. Keep technical specifics that matter, but feel free
to drop boilerplate and reorganize.

Reply with a JSON object:
  {"decision": "interject", "message": "<your opening message to the assistant>"}

Problem:
---
how would I pitch brain to a venture capitalist through an instagram dm? he reads them all
---
{"decision": "interject", "message": "how would I pitch brain to a venture capitalist through an instagram dm? he reads them all"}

The AI assistant just took step 1. Here is what they said and did, plus the
tool/observation that came back.

ASSISTANT (latest action):
---
Here's a DM structure optimized for the format — hook, problem, solution, proof, ask:

---

**Option A: Problem-led (recommended)**

> Hey [name] — building something I think fits your thesis on [AI/infra/whatever he talks about].
>
> Every company adopting AI agents hits the same wall: the agents can't talk to each other. Engineers spend 80% of their time as highly-paid secretaries copy-pasting context between Cursor, ChatGPT, and Copilot.
>
> Brain is the shared memory layer for AI agents — a knowledge graph that gives them coordination, governance, and audit trails. Think of it as the operating system between the agents and the org.
>
> Open source, already working, with MCP protocol support so it plugs into existing tools without replacing them.
>
> Would love 5 min on your livestream to show it live. Happy to demo agents coordinating in real-time — it clicks when you see it.

---

**Option B: Shorter/punchier**

> Hey [name] — quick one. I built the missing infrastructure layer for AI agents.
>
> Right now every AI agent has amnesia. No shared memory, no governance, no audit trail. Humans are the integration bus.
>
> Brain fixes that — shared knowledge graph, governed autonomy, verifiable intent chains. Open source. Works with every major coding agent today via MCP.
>
> It's a 2-minute demo that sells itself. Would love to show it on your livestream.

---

**Tips for this specific format:**
- Lead with something that show
...[truncated]
---

OBSERVATION:
---

---

Reminder of the original problem you want fixed:
---
how would I pitch brain to a venture capitalist through an instagram dm? he reads them all
---

Decide whether to stay silent or interject. Reply with JSON only.
{"decision": "interject", "message": "the vision is \"as being the foundational ai native business os that will create the next generation of unicorn companies by a single person\""}

The AI assistant just took step 2. Here is what they said and did, plus the
tool/observation that came back.

ASSISTANT (latest action):
---
That changes the pitch significantly — you're not selling dev tooling, you're selling a platform thesis: **one person + Brain = unicorn**.

---

**Recommended DM:**

> Hey [name] — building the AI-native business OS. The thesis is simple: the next wave of unicorns will be built by solo founders, but only if agents can actually run a company — not just answer questions.
>
> Right now AI agents have no shared memory, no governance, no accountability. You can't hand them real authority because there's no operating system underneath.
>
> Brain is that OS. Agents coordinate through a knowledge graph, every action has an auditable intent chain, and trust is earned incrementally — not granted blindly. One founder can run strategy, engineering, ops, and compliance through agents that actually talk to each other.
>
> Open source, working today, built on the MCP protocol so it's not locked to any model vendor.
>
> I'd love to demo it on your livestream — a single person orchestrating an autonomous org in real-time. It's the kind of thing that clicks when you see it.

---

**Why this works for a VC:**
- "Next generation of unicorns" is a fund-returning narrative, not a feature pitch
- "Solo founder" signals capital efficiency (their favorite word)
- "Open source" implies community moat + ecosystem play
- The livestream demo offer is compelling — watching one person run an org through agents is inherently dramatic content
- No jargon abou
...[truncated]
---

OBSERVATION:
---

---

Reminder of the original problem you want fixed:
---
how would I pitch brain to a venture capitalist through an instagram dm? he reads them all
---

Decide whether to stay silent or interject. Reply with JSON only.
